skill.name: c-pro
skill.description: Write efficient C code with proper memory management, pointer arithmetic, and system calls. Handles embedded systems, kernel modules, and performance-critical code. Use PROACTIVELY for C optimization, memory issues, or system programming.
skill.summary: Guide C systems programming with memory safety, POSIX practices, debugging, and performance checks.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed both static findings as false positives. The backticked text at SKILL.md line 26 is Markdown documentation, not shell execution, and line 3 contains a C programming description with no weak cryptographic algorithm.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The text at SKILL.md line 26 says to open a referenced Markdown playbook if examples are needed. It is not a shell command, does not execute code, and provides no command injection path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks are Markdown formatting around a file path. No shell invocation, command runner, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The text at SKILL.md line 3 describes C programming topics such as memory management and pointer arithmetic. No cryptographic function, algorithm, or security protocol is referenced.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line contains natural-language metadata only. There is no evidence of weak cryptographic algorithm use.

content.value_statement: C systems work can fail through leaks, unchecked calls, and unclear ownership. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward safer C implementations and verification steps.
actual_capabilities: Guides C99 and C11 code design with explicit memory ownership.
Prompts for error handling around malloc, system calls, and resource cleanup.
Supports POSIX systems programming, pthreads, and embedded constraints.
Encourages static analysis with clang-tidy and runtime checks with valgrind.
Frames outputs around source files, headers, Makefiles, tests, and benchmarks.
limitations: Does not provide a compiler, debugger, or runtime environment by itself.
Cannot prove memory safety without building and testing the generated code.
Only covers C-focused workflows and related systems programming topics.
Referenced supplemental resources may not be present in every package copy.

use_cases.title: Implement POSIX Utilities
use_cases.description: Create small C tools with checked system calls, clear cleanup paths, and portable build flags.

use_cases.title: Review Embedded C
use_cases.description: Improve stack usage, allocation choices, and error handling for resource-limited firmware code.

use_cases.title: Debug Memory Issues
use_cases.description: Plan leak checks, ownership fixes, and valgrind validation for unstable C programs.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Small C Program
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a C11 command-line program for [task]. Include clear ownership rules, checked return values, and a simple Makefile.

prompt_templates.title: Add Error Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this C function for unchecked returns, leaks, and cleanup errors. Explain each issue and provide a corrected version.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Systems Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this performance-critical C code. Identify bottlenecks, preserve correctness, and recommend profiling steps before changes.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Concurrent Module
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a pthread-based C module for [requirements]. Define synchronization, ownership, failure handling, tests, and valgrind checks.

output_examples.input: Create a file-copy utility in C.
output_examples.output: A C11 implementation plan with checked open, read, write, and close calls, plus cleanup rules and build flags.

output_examples.input: Review a parser that leaks memory on error paths.
output: Validation steps using compiler warnings and valgrind.

output_examples.output: A bounded design that avoids dynamic allocation, defines overflow behavior, and includes focused unit tests.

best_practices: State ownership for every allocated buffer and resource handle.
Compile with strict warnings and run static analysis before optimization.
Verify memory behavior with tests, valgrind, or equivalent tooling.
anti_patterns: Do not ignore malloc, fopen, pthread, or system call failures.
Do not optimize before measuring performance with a realistic workload.
Do not mix ownership responsibilities across callers without documentation.

faq.question: What C standards does this skill target?
faq.answer: It asks for C99 or C11 compatible code, with POSIX guidance when system calls are required.

faq.question: Can it write complete C projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide source files, headers, Makefiles, tests, and verification steps.

faq.question: Does it run valgrind or gdb?
faq.answer: No. It recommends how to use those tools, but execution depends on the host environment.

faq.question: Is it useful for embedded systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for resource constraints, stack usage, and limited allocation.

faq.question: Can it help with kernel modules?
faq.answer: It can provide C systems guidance, but kernel code still needs expert review and target-specific testing.

faq.question: Does it replace a C security review?
faq.answer: No. It improves code quality guidance, but high-risk C code still needs human security review.
